lede: Today's a wild one. GitHub Copilot started injecting ads into pull requests, then backed down after major backlash. Meanwhile, Sora is apparently struggling, and someone built a language model trained entirely on Victorian-era books. Plus a helpful tool to cut Claude's output tokens and an interactive way to learn Claude Code by doing.

quick_links.title: Claude Code's source code leaked via NPM map file

quick_links.note: PSA if you're using Claude Code heavily
quick_links.title: Claude Code cache bug can 10-20x your API costs
Stanford study: AI vision models invent images they never see

items.body: Developers discovered that GitHub Copilot was editing promotional content directly into their pull request suggestions. The backlash was swift and loud — within hours, GitHub killed the feature. It's a good reminder that even big tech companies can misread the room spectacularly. Popular discussion on HN.
items.title: GitHub Copilot injected ads into pull requests, then reversed course

items.body: WSJ reports that OpenAI's video generation tool isn't finding traction despite being first to market. Turns out being early doesn't guarantee success when competitors catch up fast. Worth reading if you're thinking about product strategy in fast-moving AI markets.
items.title: Sora's struggling after a year on the market

items.body: This GitHub repo provides a reusable configuration file that makes Claude's responses more concise and token-efficient. Drop it in your project and Claude will be less chatty by default. Simple but useful if you're burning through API credits. Discussion on HN.
items.title: Cut Claude's output tokens with a universal claude.md file

items.body: An interactive learning site that teaches you Claude Code through hands-on exercises. If you've been meaning to try Claude's coding features but don't want to wade through docs, this is a good starting point. HN discussion here.
items.title: Learn Claude Code by doing, not reading

items.body: Trip Venturella built a language model using only out-of-copyright books from the British Library — over 28,000 Victorian texts. It's not going to replace GPT-4, but it's a fascinating experiment in what you can train without modern data. You can run it locally with Simon's llm-mrchatterbox plugin.
items.title: Mr. Chatterbox: A language model trained entirely on Victorian-era text

items.body: A developer used an AI agent to convert a hand-drawn sketch into a fully functional 3D-printed pegboard for their kid. The repo shows the whole workflow. It's a nice example of using AI for practical, one-off design tasks. Show HN discussion.
items.title: Turn a sketch into a 3D-printable pegboard with an AI agent

slack_text:
☕ Good morning, NOLA! Tuesday, March 31, 2026. Today's vibe: chaos and course corrections.

---

🔥 *Big Moves*

• GitHub really stepped in it: <https://notes.zachmanson.com/copilot-edited-an-ad-into-my-pr/|Copilot started injecting ads into pull requests>, then <https://www.theregister.com/2026/03/30/github_copilot_ads_pull_requests/|quickly reversed course> after developers lost their minds. Even big tech companies can completely misread the room.

• Meanwhile, <https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/the-sudden-fall-of-openais-most-hyped-product-since-chatgpt-64c730c9|WSJ reports Sora is struggling> a year after launch. First-mover advantage doesn't mean much when the competition catches up fast.

---

🛠️ *Tools Worth Trying*

• If you're burning through Claude tokens, check out <https://github.com/drona23/claude-token-efficient|this universal claude.md file> that makes responses more concise by default. Simple config, real savings.

• Want to learn Claude Code? <https://claude.nagdy.me/|This interactive site> teaches you through hands-on exercises instead of docs. Much better way to learn.

• This is delightful: <https://simonwillison.net/2026/Mar/30/mr-chatterbox/#atom-everything|Mr. Chatterbox is a language model> trained entirely on Victorian-era books from the British Library. You can run it locally. It's not powerful, but it's a cool experiment.

---

🎨 *Things People Built*

• Someone used an AI agent to <https://github.com/virpo/pegboard|turn a sketch into a 3D-printed pegboard> for their kid. The whole workflow is in the repo — nice example of using AI for practical, one-off design tasks.
